title:
The General Tax Authority Participates in the University of Doha for Science and Technology
                                    Career Fair
description: The General Tax Authority (GTA) participated in the University of Doha for Science and Technology (UDST) career fair on November 20th and 21st, with the aim of communicating with talented graduates seeking careers in the tax sector, as part of its keenness to attract national cadres in a manner corresponding with its job vacancy needs, in the context of prioritizing work and contributing to the development and establishment of human forces at the scientific and practical level, in accordance with the training programs available to GTA employees. During its participation in the fair, the GTA presented its plans and programs, as well as introducing the Authority’s main functions and services. The Human Resources Department staff that took part in the fair, also provided an overview of the Authority’s role in economic development, including programs tailored to new employees to develop their technical and functional capacity, in line with the needs of the tax sector. The Authority is committed to participating in career fairs organized by various national educational institutions, in line with its direction to align its human resources strategy to help attract young Qatari talent and prepare future experts to contribute to the development of Qatar’s tax system that is consistent with Qatar National Vision 2030.

title:
The General Tax Authority Organizes an Awareness Campaign for World Diabetes Day in
                                    Collaboration with the Qatar Diabetes Association
description: On Sunday, November 19, 2023, the General Tax Authority (GTA), in collaboration with the Qatar Diabetes Association (QDA), organized an awareness campaign to mark World Diabetes Day. The initiative aimed at enhancing the quality of life for GTA employees, promoting healthy lifestyle practices, and underscore the significance of early diabetes detection and regular monitoring to mitigate its severe complications. The QDA medical team provided GTA employees with health tips and performed comprehensive screenings, including blood sugar level checks, weight and height measurements to identify diabetes risk factors. The team also provided the participants with personalized nutrition consultations and guidance on adopting a healthy lifestyle to combat diabetes. The event also included educational materials that were handed to participants to enhance their understanding of the disease and its possible complications, such as heart attack, stroke, kidney failure, retinopathy, and nerve damage. Additionally, the medical team addressed attendees’ inquiries regarding the various types of diabetes, early signs, and preventive measures. This event stems from GTA’s keenness to harness global health initiatives via engaging in awareness-raising activities that help promote health consciousness among its employees, particularly regarding serious diseases that adversely affect quality of life. Notably, the Qatar Diabetes Association, a member of the Qatar Foundation for Education, Science and Community Development (QF), supports individuals and families living with diabetes to assist with its management.

title:
The General Tax Authority Organizes a Briefing Session to Introduce Programs of the
                                    Doha Institute for Graduate Studies at the Authority’s Headquarters
description: In collaboration with the Doha Institute for Graduate Studies, the General Tax Authority organized a briefing session at the GTA headquarters. The session featured a detailed presentation introducing DI’s academic programs and highlighted its role in preparing researchers capable of contributing to the production of knowledge, in alignment with international standards.

title:
The General Tax Authority Receives a Delegation From the State Tax Administration
                                    of the People’s Republic of China
description: His Excellency Mr. Ahmed bin Eisa Al-Mohannadi, President of the General Tax Authority, received the delegation of the People’s Republic of China represented by His Excellency Mr. Wang Jun, Commissioner of the State Tax Administration, and the accompanying delegation. The meeting discussed international tax related topics, as well as exchanging views on common-interest tax issues with the Chinese party, and ways to enhance mutual cooperation in this respect. This visit comes in the context of enhancing joint cooperation and exchanging tax expertise to serve the tax policies between the two countries.

title:
The General Tax Authority Hosts a Regional Workshop on the Global Anti-Base Erosion
                                    Rules
description: The General Tax Authority Hosts a Regional Workshop on the Global Anti-Base Erosion Rules The General Tax Authority hosted a regional workshop, titled “Regional Workshop on the Global Anti-Base Erosion Rules: In-Depth Discussion on the Design and Implementation of DMTT”, at Hilton Doha Hotel from October 30 to 31, 2023. The event saw the participation of over 150 attendees, covering representatives the GCC countries, in addition to ministries, institutions and the private sector, and stakeholders within the tax sector in Qatar. On the first day of the workshop, participants received an overview of recent developments related to the Global Anti-Base Erosion Model Rules (GloBE Rules) and the Qualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (QDMTT). The day also featured discussions on economic impact assessments, along with a presentation that covered the elements to consider in the design and implementation of a QDMTT. Commenting on hosting the regional workshop in Qatar, H.E. Ahmed bin Issa Al-Mohannadi, President of the General Tax Authority, stated: “This regional workshop carries significant importance as it sheds light on the decision to implement Pillar 2 and enact the global minimum corporate tax rate for Multinational Enterprises (MNEs), in addition to the in-depth discussions on the design and execution of a Qualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (QDMTT), aiming to raise awareness about the need to combat tax-base erosion and profit shifting, which will ultimately contribute to promoting Qatar's appeal as a global investment destination.” And he added: “We invite you to make the most of this workshop by actively engaging in the multiple sessions, and we anticipate your invaluable contributions during the sub-workshops, which will offer a unique platform for interaction and exchange of experiences concerning the design and implementation of Pillar 2.” This regional workshop carries significant importance as it sheds light on the decision to implement Pillar 2 and enact the global minimum corporate tax rate for Multinational Enterprises (MNEs), in addition to the in-depth discussions on the design and execution of a Qualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (QDMTT), aiming to raise awareness about the need to combat tax-base erosion and profit shifting, which will ultimately contribute to promoting Qatar's appeal as a global investment destination.” He added: “We invite you to make the most of this workshop by actively engaging in the multiple sessions, and we anticipate your invaluable contributions during the sub-workshops, which will offer a unique platform for interaction and exchange of experiences concerning the design and implementation of Pillar 2.” The second day featured focused sub-workshops on the design and implementation of QDMTT, with active participation from attendees who shared their relevant experiences. The General Tax Authority's hosting of the regional workshop aligns with its mission to promote tax transparency, facilitate information exchange for tax purposes among participating nations, bridge gaps in today’s interconnected tax systems, and strengthen international economic cooperation. This approach is intended to yield mutual benefits, uphold the Anti-Base Erosion Rules, and prevent double taxation. In addition, the General Tax Authority’s diversification contributes to the realization of the Qatar National Vision 2030 objectives, which are centered on achieving sustainable development, promoting economic diversification, and ensuring a prosperous future for generations to come.

title:
The General Tax Authority Delegation visits the Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority in
                                    the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
description: A delegation from the General Tax Authority visited the Zakat, Tax, and Customs Authority in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia on Tuesday, October 24, 2023. During the visit, various topics of common interest in the tax field were discussed. In addition, the two parties reviewed the latest developments in global tax systems, the tax services provided and their working mechanism. This visit comes within the framework of the ongoing cooperation between the brotherly countries, with the aim of exchanging views and tax expertise.

title:
The General Tax Authority President Attends the Opening Ceremony of the Entrepreneurship
                                    for Sustainability & Impact (ESI) 6th International Conference
description: On October 17, 2023, H.E. Ahmed bin Issa Al-Mohannadi, President of the General Tax Authority (GTA), joined a distinguished gathering of Excellencies and dignitaries to participate in the opening ceremony of the Entrepreneurship for Sustainability & Impact (ESI) 6th International Conference. The event enjoys the esteemed patronage of H.E. Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani, Qatar's Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs. The conference was hosted by the Center for Entrepreneurship & Organizational Excellence (CEOE) at the College of Business and Economics at Qatar University. The conference, organized under the theme "Tackling Grand Challenges for a Sustainable Future" aims to facilitate collaboration, promote the exchange of knowledge in the fields of entrepreneurship and innovation, and bridge the gap between the academic community and policymakers. Its core mission is to contribute to the development of policies capable of effectively addressing today’s intricate economic, social, and environmental challenges. The GTA's participation reflects its commitment to fortifying partnerships with renowned academic institutions and highlights its key role in championing the advancement of a sustainable economy. It is worth nothing that the Entrepreneurship for Sustainability & Impact (ESI) is an annual conference organized by the College of Business and Economics at Qatar University. A leading entrepreneurship event in the MENA region, ESI brings together a community of scholars and practitioners committed to the advancement of entrepreneurship, innovation, and organizational excellence through research, teaching, and practical application.

title:
The Authority’s President Receives the President of the University of Doha for Science
                                    and Technology
description: His Excellency Mr. Ahmed bin Issa Al-Mohannadi, President of the General Tax Authority, received today, Wednesday, September 27, 2023, Dr. Salem Nasser Al-Naemi, President of the University of Doha for Science and Technology, alongside other representatives. During the visit, the two parties discussed aspects of mutual cooperation. This meeting demonstrates the priority accorded by the General Tax Authority to Qatar's education and economic sector, with the goal of fostering tax awareness and empowering the development of local talent to contribute to a sustainable future.

title:
The General Tax Authority Participates in the Annual Belt and Road Initiative Tax
                                    Administration Cooperation Forum
description: Within the framework of strengthening international cooperation, the General Tax Authority participated in the Annual Belt and Road Initiative Tax Administration Cooperation Forum (BRITACOF), and was represented by H.E. Mr. Ahmed bin Issa Al-Mohannadi, President of the General Tax Authority, held from 11 to 13 September in Tbilisi of Georgia. The conference formed a unique international presence platform, gathering more than 140 representatives from 33 different countries, as well as representatives from 10 prominent international organizations. The conference also shed light on several vital tax-related topics, where the need to improve the tax environment, promote digitization and optimize the use of information technology in the development of tax services were also discussed. Practical solutions were also reviewed to reduce the tax burden in the business environment. In addition, countries’ experiences in increasing the level of tax compliance and its positive impact on the economy were shared. The Authority’s participation in this conference reflects its commitment to enhancing transparency and international cooperation and highlights its contribution to the sustainability of domestic and international economic growth.

title: The General Tax Authority participates in the Qatar University Career Fair
description: The General Tax Authority (GTA) participated in the 16th Qatar University Career Fair, hosted from September 11 to 14, 2023, at the Student Affairs Building at Qatar University, with the aim of providing opportunities for students and graduates to explore professional opportunities within the labor market, in addition to field training and academic support opportunities. The GTA's HR team, who participated in the fair, presented an overview of the Authority and its role in economic development. In addition to providing an explanation of the training and career pathways offered by the GTA and answering students’ inquiries regarding the employment landscape within the tax sector. They also responded to inquiries on how these job opportunities allow graduates to fulfill their ambitions and map their future career trajectories. The General Tax Authority’s participation in the QU Career Fair aligns with its strategic vision to advance the Human Development pillar of the Qatar National Vision 2030, by introducing career opportunities within the tax sector among national talent and amplifying efforts to promote Qatarization. This, in turn, provides the nation with a pool of highly skilled and specialized professionals in this field.

title: Launching the "No Objection to Transfer of Ownership" service through Dhareeba portal.
description: The General Tax Authority has announced the closure of its outlets in the government service centers, and to facilitate the completion of taxpayers’ transactions easily and smoothly; taxpayers can submit a no-objection request to transfer ownership, through Dhareeba portal. This service is dedicated to changing the shares of partners in a (specific) company by selling all or part of the shares to one of the partners registered within the commercial registration, or to new partners. This service is an important connection between the General Tax Authority and its partners from the relevant government agencies and taxpayers, as this service allows taxpayers to calculate and process the various types of tax and it assists them in managing their tax transactions, in accordance with the Qatari tax laws. Dhareeba portal aims to provide the best tax services and have these services available in an effective and accessible way. It helps, taxpayers to electronically manage their transactions, which will contribute to achieve better results in general for the country. Taxpayer can access "Dhareeba" online portal and complete the NOC request and submit two types of NOC requests - change of ownership and tax clearance. In addition, taxpayers can follow up on their queries through the single window at the Ministry of Commerce and Industry. For more inquiries, you can call 16565 from within Qatar, and 00974 44069941 from outside Qatar.

title: Joint Workshop between the General Tax Authority and OECD
description: The General Tax Authority (GTA) organized a joint technical workshop with the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD), with the participation of representatives from the concerned public agencies and private sector stakeholders to discuss the proposed changes to international tax rules to address tax challenges arising from digitization of the economy. GTA stressed that the workshop was a good opportunity for all parties to exchange views, information and the best practices related to the international commitments, in order to address the challenges arising from digitization, and the mechanism of applying Pillar One on digital companies and Pillar Two on companies with foreign branches. "Despite the great interest in promoting international cooperation on improving tax laws and regulations, we still need to find a balance between our endeavors to enhance the flexibility of commercial activities, increase tax revenues and ensure tax continuity, on the one hand, and the need to provide attractive tax investments that encourage the private sector, on the other hand. This has become very important, and it requires careful studies to come up with tax systems that allow the flexibility required for better private sector investments, while simultaneously increasing tax revenues to support national economic plans and enable better public spending,” GTA added.

title: GTA Explains Compliance Cases Related to Excise Tax
description: The General Tax Authority (GTA) plays a key role in driving Qatar's economic development and achieving the relevant objectives of National Vision 2030, specifically strengthening the national economy, supporting the general budget, diversifying revenues and reducing reliance on hydrocarbon resources. The General Tax Authority (GTA) is responsible for overseeing the enforcement of tax laws, including Law No. (25) of 2018 on Excise Tax, which came into effect at the beginning of January this year. This law applies to a range of unhealthy products, imposing a 100% tax on tobacco products, energy drinks, and special purpose goods, while a 50% tax is levied on carbonated beverages. The Excise Tax serves as an instrument for strengthening Qatar's economy and represents a strategic investment in public health. It is designed to effectively reduce the overall consumption of harmful products and promote a sustainable, healthy lifestyle. Additionally, the revenues generated from this tax are utilized to enhance funding for healthcare, education, infrastructure, and other essential initiatives that contribute to the well-being of future generations. The General Tax Authority (GTA) mandates that all businesses engaged in the import, production, or storage of excise goods submit a one-time transitional tax return. This return must accurately declare all excise goods held in inventory as of the day prior to the effective date of the Excise Tax Law, which is January 1st. The GTA also clarified that there is no minimum threshold for filing an Excise Tax return. All businesses holding excise goods in inventory are required to submit a transitional tax return no later than January 31st. Furthermore, these businesses must pay any excise tax due within 30 days of submitting the transitional return. The GTA mandates that businesses engaged in the import or production of goods, as well as those intending to operate an excise tax warehouse, register with the Authority within the specified timeframe. These entities are also required to maintain accurate books and accounting records, file quarterly tax returns, and pay any applicable excise tax on a quarterly basis. The law provides a 90-day window from its effective date for businesses involved in the import or local production of excise goods to update their tax registration status in compliance with the new regulations. For businesses commencing activities that require tax registration after the law's effective date, registration is mandatory at least 30 days prior to commencing such activities. Businesses and enterprises that maintain a stock of excise goods solely for commercial purposes—such as hotels, retail shops, and tobacco stores—and do not engage in importing, producing, or operating an excise tax warehouse are not required to register with the GTA for excise tax purposes. However, these businesses remain liable for a one-time transitional tax on their existing inventory and must submit a transitional tax return. The commercial establishments and businesses engaged in the production, import, or storage of specific goods are eligible to seek an excise tax warehouse license. This license permits the authorized entity to store or manufacture such goods within a designated location under a tax suspension arrangement. The licensee is required to settle the excise tax obligations for any excisable goods departing from their tax warehouse on a quarterly basis. The GTA stated that Law No. (25) of 2018 on Excise Tax establishes penalties for tax non-compliance and tax evasion. These penalties include imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year and fines up to three times the amount of tax owed, or either of these penalties, as outlined in Articles (16) and (18) of the law.
